text: Well, I chose to perform a posterior unipolar approach for a non-anatomical posterior segment resection of the left upper lobe for a tumor 1.1 cm comacellular carcinoma at one multimorbide female patient.
Posterior approach allows, to my opinion, better exposure of the posterior hilum structures and therefore I find it very suitable for posterior segment sections.
The fissure was easily divided.
In some cases you can simply undermine it from behind and staple it rather than dissect it.

now I'm dissecting the branch for segment 2 posterior segment branch and I'm about to take it with the stapling device I'm looking for the bronchus for segment 2 like always I had it checked via bronchoscopy perioperatively

now due to an unfavorable angle I wasn't able to place the stapling device and this is one trick of mine I'm utilizing Robinson 20 charrier chest tube as a guidance it works all the time so inflation test was not efficient
you can see the remaining bronchus for segment 1.
As I said inflation test was not efficient so we had to rely on our idea about the borders of the posterior segment and of course the location of tumor.

again this was insufficient so we had to proceed with the resection the primary objective was an adjacent segment so the first thing to do was obviously taking off the segmentary pay your attention now please
So my first thought was that bleeding arose due to a tear on the artery lateral wall and that again might have happened due to over traction of the branch.
So the first thing to do is to have this bleeding under control and once I had it,

I order for a trichotomy set to be prepared it usually takes a bit of time but before trichotomy I want to check up again how did it look like and now it became obvious that there was no tear on the artery wall but a gap on the branch
So I decided to try to solve it by placing a clip on it.
I'm utilizing Covidian MultiFire 10mm clip.
This one was successful also previously on several occasion and it holds quite good.
However, it has this unfavorable characteristic to slip off on mechanical or physical stimulation.
So that is why I decided not to dissect further in that region, but simply to complete the lobectomy.
So segment atries were divided one after another.
It goes pretty straightforward.
The fissure was completed toward mediastinum.
I'm encircling the bronchus right now and the last structure that need to be taken was upper lobe vein
